name: Steamed & Chilled Clams W/lime, Chive, & Mustard Mayonnaise
recipeIngredient: ¾ cup Oil (corn, peanut, or other mild vegetable oil, NOT olive)
¼ teaspoon Lime zest (colored rind only, no pith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the clams by boiling half an inch of water in a large pot.
- Add well-scrubbed clams and cover the pot.
- Bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Uncover the pot (to prevent boiling over) and continue cooking,
shaking or stirring occasionally, for 2-3 minutes, until all clams have
opened.
3. Transfer the cooled clams into a collander set over a bowl; reserve the broth.
- Strain the broth through cheesecloth into a separate container,
stopping before any sand appears.
4. Stir and swish the chilled clams through the strained broth to further desand them.
- Lift out the clams with a slotted spoon and set aside.
5. Chill the clams in the refrigerator or freeze for later use.
6. Just before serving, drain the clams again and fold them into about half the mayonnaise.
- Refrigerate until ready to serve.
7. Prepare the Lime, Chive, & Mustard Mayonnaise:
- Whisk together egg yolk, lime juice, and mustard.
- Gradually whisk in oil a few drops at a time, beating well after each addition.
- Once thickened, continue adding remaining oil in a thin stream while beating rapidly.
- Season with salt, pepper, chives, lime zest, and Tabasco; taste carefully and adjust as needed.
8. Serve the sauced clams in seafood cocktail glasses or small bowls,
topped with more mayonnaise and garnished with fresh green herbs like parsley, coriander, watercress, or baby lettuce.
Note: Use corn, peanut, or other mild vegetable oil for best results; avoid olive oil.
---
